mechanism: Flowing mixture of water and volcanic debris moving through confined river valley. Ground vibration from mass momentum (infrasonic); low-frequency rumble from turbulent flow; boulder grinding and material crushing at close range.
status: Second entry in GEOPHYSICS ARC. Lahar as the destructive potential of the volcanic system documented at degree 261. E=7 is the Geophysics Arc maximum. Real hazard zones: Orting, Puyallup, Sumner, Auburn, Kent.

lahar_physics.definition: Flowing mixture of water and volcanic debris. Consistency of wet concrete at high concentrations. Fluid and fast-moving.
water_sources: Glacial melt (triggered by volcanic heat or eruption)
lahar_physics.flow_characteristics: Dense slurry carrying boulders (Osceola transported boulders of thousands of tons). Initial flow front most dangerous. Progressively dilutes downstream.

osceola_mudflow.volume_comparison: Approximately 2-3x volume of Mt. St. Helens 1980 eruption
osceola_mudflow.source: Eastern flank collapse of Mt. Rainier triggered by hydrothermal weakening of volcanic edifice
osceola_mudflow.path: White River valley → Puget Sound lowlands near Tacoma
osceola_mudflow.current_land_use: Modern agricultural lowlands of Puyallup River delta and much of the Kent Valley are built on Osceola deposit

hazard_zones.details: Entirely within lahar inundation zone. Built on Osceola deposit. Warning time 30-45 minutes. Vertical evacuation planning. Evacuation drills conducted. Signage to high ground.
Puyallup River valley primary lahar pathway. Valley-floor portions in hazard zone.
Adjacent to Puyallup on valley floor. Similar hazard profile.
White River lahar pathway. Industrial/commercial infrastructure in inundation zones.
Near Puyallup River delta. Fuel storage in potential inundation area.
Between White River and Green River valleys. Near edge of some hazard mapping.

monitoring.method: Automated sensors in river channels detecting sudden flow change. Automated alert to emergency management. Integrated with summit volcanic monitoring.
monitoring.note: Lahar detection network and volcanic monitoring are two components of the same integrated early warning system.

acoustic_sequence.description: Low-frequency ground vibration (infrasonic). Primary signal from mass momentum of flow. Detectable before visual arrival.
Low-frequency rumble. Turbulent mixing of solid and liquid components. Often compared to fast freight train, lower in pitch, more sustained.
Turbulent flow noise. Boulders grinding against bedrock and each other. Waves within the flow. Material crushing and breaking. E=7.

theory_nodes.concept: Lahar as the most destructive volcanic hazard in the PNW. Not lava flows (too slow), not pyroclastic flows (too localized in most Rainier scenarios), but lahars -- because of the enormous ice volume on Rainier and the dense urban populations in lahar pathways. Osceola Mudflow (3.8 cubic km, ~5,600 years ago) reached Puget Sound. Modern lahar would bury Orting, Puyallup, Sumner, Auburn, Kent under volcanic debris.
theory_nodes.description: Lahar as primary PNW volcanic hazard: specific reasons why.

theory_nodes.concept: Orting as the acute case: entirely within lahar inundation zone, built on Osceola deposit, warning time 30-45 minutes (insufficient for vehicle evacuation in modern traffic), vertical evacuation planning. Orting is the most completely documented case of a community whose existence is contingent on warning time from a volcanic monitoring network.
theory_nodes.description: Orting: acute lahar hazard case study.

theory_nodes.concept: Geophysics Arc internal escalation: degree 261 (background tremor, E=5) to degree 262 (destructive event, E=7). The same volcanic system, two acoustic registers: the normal and the catastrophic. The monitoring of degree 261 exists to detect the event of degree 262. E=7 is the Geophysics Arc maximum.
theory_nodes.description: Volcanic arc escalation: background monitoring to catastrophic event.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 equilibrium: Lahar Simulation Rumble (E=7) + Gas Huffer (S36 262, E=7) = E=14 combined. Both are unstoppable forward-moving forces. The lahar follows drainage downhill; Gas Huffer follows the garage rock formula. Both are large, physical, direction-locked.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 perfect equilibrium: lahar and garage rock as parallel unstoppable forces.

cross_references.relationship: VOLCANIC ARC. Background tremor (261, E=5) to destructive event (262, E=7). The monitoring system at 261 exists to detect the 262 event. Same volcanic system, two acoustic registers.
GEOPHYSICS ARC continues. Volcanic (262) to seismic (263). Ground vibration mechanisms differ: lahar mass flow vs. fault rupture seismic wave.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. PERFECT EQUILIBRIUM. Both are unstoppable forward-moving forces. The lahar does not stop; the Gas Huffer song does not stop. Full-volume forward momentum as the shared mode.
